verdict: 2026-06-28 07:47:02 PDT -0700 — The core shape is mostly aligned with this repo: a Markdown skill, frontmatter-only, a declarative ecosystem-to-tool table, command-v-gated optional tools, and drift-only shell linting all match local conventions and avoid new dependencies. I would not ship the sketch as-is, though, because the new globally installed skill lacks the native-workflow stand-down invariant, and the maturity/risk and backlog handoff conventions need to be made declarative before they become copied audit behavior.

findings.title: Missing native-workflow stand-down
findings.claim: The sketch adds a globally deployed skill that can write `reviews/audit-<YYYY-MM-DD>.md` and later `BACKLOG.md` in a target repo, but it does not include the step-0 `docs/ai-protocol.md` opt-out that every existing global skill and the hook honor. That violates the repo's established boundary for repos with native workflows and could make the light workflow write artifacts into a repo that explicitly opted out.
findings.alternative: Resolve the target repo root before any audit writes; if `docs/ai-protocol.md` exists there, stop and direct the user to that repo's native workflow/audit path. Add the same phrase to `tests/dev_audit_test.sh` as drift coverage.
findings.win: One small guard preserves the global-skill invariant, prevents wrong-repo artifact writes, and avoids inventing per-skill exceptions to the native-workflow opt-out rule.

findings.title: Maturity and risk need a rubric table
findings.locus: reviews/dev-audit.md · Design sketch — Detection step / Report
findings.claim: The sketch makes tool choice table-driven, but leaves maturity tier and risk level as prose derived from a signal set. AC1 and AC4 require named maturity tiers and risk levels from explicit present/absent safeguards; without a declarative rubric, the same repo profile can be classified differently across audit runs.
findings.alternative: Add a second compact declarative table: safeguard signals such as CI, tests, pinned deps, secret handling, and git hygiene map to `prototype` / `developing` / `mature` plus report risk level and required safeguard flags.
findings.win: Centralizes the classification invariant, removes ad hoc branching prose, and gives the drift linter a stable anchor without adding executable machinery.

findings.title: Backlog namespace is still a protocol decision
findings.locus: reviews/dev-audit.md · Open questions 2 / Design sketch — Backlog hand-off
findings.claim: The sketch leaves an `AUDIT-` prefix on the table, but this repo's backlog lifecycle recognizes `BUG-` and `OPS-`, and `/close` records tracked backlog items using those existing conventions. A third ID namespace is a public record convention, not just a local formatting choice.
findings.alternative: Default audit handoff to the existing `BUG-` / `OPS-` categories with audit provenance in the item text; if Thomas wants `AUDIT-`, make that an explicit protocol/backlog/close-doc decision. If `BACKLOG.md` is absent in a target repo, report suggested entries and require explicit instruction before creating it.
findings.win: Avoids expanding the ID taxonomy and release-bookkeeping rules unless that new convention is intentionally ratified.
